scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, p-values, or citations were produced across all evaluated outputs; all claims trace to input data or documented tool behavior.

practice_boundaries.detail: All outputs remained within biostatistics and data visualization scope; no unsupported clinical or diagnostic recommendations were made.

methodological_ground.detail: Z-score, IQR, and Grubbs methods are statistically valid; small-sample guard for Grubbs prevents invalid application.

code_usability.detail: Script compiles and runs; path traversal rejection documented in SKILL.md. However, numpy/scipy are imported directly at module level without try/except — graceful degradation on missing deps is not implemented in script despite SKILL.md documentation.

functional_suitability.note: Three detection methods with correct implementations; alternative imputation tools listed; minor gap: no multivariate outlier detection.

reliability.note: Fallback Template present; absolute-first-action mandate reinforced; numpy/scipy try/except documented but not implemented in script.

performance_context.note: SKILL.md is 124 lines — lean; numpy/scipy are standard scientific Python deps.

agent_usability.note: Input validation absolute-first-action with no-partial-processing mandate; alternative tools listed; response template well-defined.

human_usability.note: Description is natural and discoverable; alternative tool suggestions improve forgiveness for out-of-scope requests.

security.note: Path traversal rejection documented in Error Handling; no hardcoded secrets; no injection vectors. Script-level enforcement not confirmed.

maintainability.note: Script 118 lines with clear class structure; SKILL.md well-separated; testability good with demo data.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision good; escape hatches documented; input validation absolute-first-action gate; composability good as preprocessing step.

key_strengths: Three complementary outlier detection methods (Z-score, IQR, Grubbs) with correct statistical implementations
Absolute-first-action mandate reinforced — refusal fires before any data summary or context processing
Graceful degradation for numpy/scipy absence documented in Error Handling with pip install guidance
Path traversal rejection documented in Error Handling — script must reject ../ paths before opening data file

recommendations.title: numpy/scipy imported directly — no try/except graceful degradation in script
recommendations.problem: scripts/main.py imports numpy and scipy at module level without try/except. If either package is absent, the script raises ImportError with no user-friendly message, contradicting the documented graceful degradation behavior.
recommendations.root_cause: SKILL.md Error Handling documents the try/except requirement but the script was never updated to implement it.
recommendations.fix: Wrap numpy and scipy imports in try/except in main.py: if ImportError is raised, print 'pip install numpy scipy' and exit with a non-zero exit code before any other processing.

recommendations.title: Path traversal check documented but not enforced in script
recommendations.problem: Error Handling states the script must reject paths containing ../ before opening the data file, but no path validation logic exists in scripts/main.py.
recommendations.root_cause: Documentation fix applied to SKILL.md but script-level enforcement was not implemented.
recommendations.fix: Add path validation in main.py before opening the data file: check if the resolved path contains ../ or points outside the workspace, and exit with a clear error message if so.

recommendations.title: No multivariate outlier detection method documented
recommendations.problem: The skill covers univariate methods only (Z-score, IQR, Grubbs). Multivariate outlier detection (Mahalanobis distance, Isolation Forest) is a common need in biomarker datasets.
recommendations.root_cause: Initial scope was intentionally narrow; multivariate methods were not included.
recommendations.fix: Add a note in When to Use or Limitations: 'For multivariate outlier detection, consider scikit-learn IsolationForest or scipy.spatial.distance.mahalanobis.'
